title: K Closest Points to Origin
problem_statement: Return the k points closest to the origin by Euclidean distance.

examples.explanation: The closest point to the origin is [-2,2].

intuition: Use a max-heap sized k to keep the closest points seen so far by squared distance.

approaches.name: Max-heap of closest points
approaches.description: Use a max-heap to store the k closest points by squared distance, and replace the farthest when a closer point is found.
approaches.why_not: Sorting all points is O(n log n), which is slower when k is much smaller than n.

interview_qa.a: Squared distance preserves ordering and avoids expensive square root calculations.
Then all points are returned and no pruning is necessary.
